# Upstream Cross-Check — Source Index

This file lists every official document used in this cross-check round.
Any finding in `REPORT.md` that lacks a `Source` entry pointing to one of these
records is considered inadmissible.

**Capture date:** 2026-05-02 (UTC).
**Evidence type:** Live HTTP — all captures generated by POSTing test vectors to the
running gateway's `/v1/debug/normalize` endpoint (active when `GATEWAY_DEBUG_NORMALIZE=1`).
The endpoint calls `normalizeGatewayRequest + buildOpenRouterRequest` server-side
and returns `{ protocol, ir, outbound }` without contacting any upstream backend.
**Evidence files:** `docs/upstream-crosscheck/captures/<ID>.outbound.json` (22 files) + `summary.json`
**Evidence run:** 2026-05-02 — 22 PASS, 0 FAIL (see `captures/summary.json`)
**Evidence generation command:** `bash scripts/upstream-crosscheck.sh` (calls debug endpoint)

---

Supplemental docs in `docs/upstream-crosscheck/aws-bedrock/`.

see architecture constraint note (§6 below).

| BED-4 | https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/bedrock-endpoints.html | #runtime-endpoints | `docs/upstream-crosscheck/aws-bedrock/runtime-endpoints.md` | 2026-05 |

---

## 6. Architecture constraint note

> The gateway currently routes **all** traffic through Friend Proxy → OpenRouter.
> AWS Bedrock native InvokeModel/Converse and GCP Vertex native generateContent
> are NOT directly called by this gateway — they are reached via OpenRouter's
> `amazon-bedrock` and `google-vertex` provider slugs.
>
> Consequently:
> - "Expected" for Bedrock/Vertex items is "correct OpenRouter provider lock",
>   not "correct Bedrock/Vertex wire format".
> - Native wire-format deviations are N-A for this gateway unless a direct
>   integration path is added in a future task.
> - The supplemental docs `aws-bedrock/*.md` and `google-vertex/*.md` are
>   included for completeness and to support future direct-integration work.

---

## 7. Evidence capture index

**Evidence type:** Live HTTP — each `.outbound.json` file is the verbatim response
from `POST http://localhost:8080/v1/debug/normalize` with the relevant test vector body.
The endpoint is active when `GATEWAY_DEBUG_NORMALIZE=1` is set in the process environment.

Each `.outbound.json` file contains:
```json
{
  "protocol": "<detected-protocol>",
  "ir": { /* GatewayRequestIR — full intermediate representation */ },
  "outbound": {
    "url": "<OpenRouter endpoint URL>",
    "headers": { /* all outbound headers */ },
    "body": { /* full outbound request body to OpenRouter */ }
  }
}
```
